what_it_does: Defines a React canvas component `Icons` (window.Icons) that browses the icon library and generates/refines icons with Vi. It declares ICON_CATEGORIES (named glyphs grouped People/Files/Communication/Architecture/etc.), reads icon bodies from window.CV_ICONS.data, and renders a searchable, category-filtered, tone-switchable grid of SVG glyphs. A generation panel calls window.CV_AI.complete with a brand line-style prompt to propose 4 icon ideas (name + inner SVG body) the user can adopt into CV_ICONS.data. Per-icon hover affordances allow refine-by-message, duplicate-as-variant, and (for session-added icons) delete.
observations: allNames is read live from Object.keys(window.CV_ICONS.data).
`generate()` prompt fixes the line style (24x24 viewBox, 1.5px stroke, rounded caps/joins, no fills, currentColor, geometric/architectural) and asks for 4 ideas as a JSON array of {name, body}.
Parses model reply as JSON array with a [...] regex fallback; filters to entries with name+body, caps at 8.
`adopt(p)` writes into window.CV_ICONS.data (mutating the shared store), de-dupes names with a random suffix, and notifies via addGenerated.
`renderIcon` colors by tone (gold/ink/bronze) and injects the body via dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
`refineIcon` revises one icon's body via CV_AI.complete and writes back; persists session icons via updateGenerated.
`duplicateIcon` creates a named variant; `deleteIcon` removes from CV_ICONS.data and removeGenerated.
Header links out to an external icon explorer at ../assets/icons/index.html.
Uses window.prompt() for the duplicate-variant description (line ~298).
Empty-search state offers a one-click path to generate the searched term with Vi.
standouts: Adoption directly mutates the live window.CV_ICONS.data so generated icons become immediately usable across the system.
Per-glyph refine and duplicate-as-variant, generating new SVG bodies that keep the line-style constraints.
Search-to-generate fallback: a no-match search becomes a seeded generation prompt.
conventions: Follows a generate -> proposals -> adopt flow.
Parses model JSON defensively (raw then [...] regex).
Routes generation through CV_AI.complete; never a raw model client.
Aliases React hooks with an `_ic` suffix for the shared bundle.
Holds icon glyph bodies in an external store (CV_ICONS.data) rather than inline.
concerns: adopt/refine/duplicate/delete directly mutate window.CV_ICONS.data and rely on parent re-render via addGenerated/updateGenerated, mixing global mutation with React state.
SVG bodies are injected via dangerouslySetInnerHTML from model output without sanitization.
Uses the blocking window.prompt() for variant input, inconsistent with the inline RefinePop used elsewhere in the file.
notes: The icon library browser: searchable/categorized glyph grid reading CV_ICONS.data, with Vi generation of 4 line-style icons and per-glyph refine/duplicate/delete.
questions: Is mutating window.CV_ICONS.data in place the intended persistence model, and does it survive reload?
How does CV_ICONS.data relate to the static ../assets/icons explorer the header links to?
What component is RefinePop (used here in 'dot' mode)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches. The header calls it an 'icon library with real AI generation' and the component browses CV_ICONS.data and generates/refines via CV_AI.
apparent_themes: Brand line-style consistency enforced in prompts
Search as a launch point for generation
intention_signals: Trying to make the icon set both browsable and elastic: keep all glyphs in one consistent line style, let a user search or generate exactly the icons they need, and refine or branch any glyph while keeping the style invariants.
novelty: Per-glyph refine and duplicate-as-variant generation plus search-becomes-generation, all writing into a live icon store.
contribution: Contributes the Icons canvas: a searchable categorized glyph grid over CV_ICONS.data, tone switching, line-style-constrained Vi generation of 4 proposals, and per-icon refine/duplicate/delete.
summary_for_embedding: A React canvas (window.Icons) browsing a categorized SVG icon library from CV_ICONS.data with search, tone switch, and Vi generation/refinement/duplication of line-style icons.
intention_for_embedding: To let users browse, search, generate, refine, and branch brand-consistent line-style icons that adopt into a live shared icon store.
